message:
fix(embed): vendor openssl + force rustls TLS for fastembed/ort/hf-hub

Cross-compile to *-musl, aarch64-linux-gnu, and x86_64-darwin in downstream
release pipelines (xf, this repo's dist) was failing because:

- fastembed v5's default features pull `ort/tls-native` and `hf-hub` defaults,
  which transitively require openssl-sys with system OpenSSL headers.
- fastembed's `hf-hub-rustls-tls` feature does NOT pass default-features=false
  to hf-hub, so hf-hub still pulls native-tls (openssl-sys) on top of rustls
  even when consumers opt into rustls.

Workspace-wide: pin fastembed and ort to rustls-only feature sets. Then,
because the hf-hub default-features quirk leaks openssl-sys back in, add an
explicit dependency on `openssl-sys` with the `vendored` feature gated
behind frankensearch-embed's `fastembed` feature. That compiles OpenSSL from
its bundled C source, sidestepping the missing system toolchain on every
cross-target while leaving non-fastembed builds unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -77,15 +77,25 @@ wide = "1.1.1"
 memmap2 = "0.9"
 
 # Embedder dependencies (feature-gated per crate)
-fastembed = "5.11.0"
+# Default features pull in native-tls (openssl-sys), which breaks
+# cross-compilation to musl/aarch64-linux/intel-darwin in downstream
+# release pipelines. Force rustls TLS via explicit features.
+fastembed = { version = "5.11.0", default-features = false, features = [
+    "ort-download-binaries-rustls-tls",
+    "hf-hub-rustls-tls",
+    "online",
+    "image-models",
+] }
 safetensors = "0.7.0"
 tokenizers = "0.22.2"
 
 # Full-text search
 tantivy = "0.25.0"
 
 # ONNX Runtime for cross-encoder reranking
-ort = "2.0.0-rc.11"
+# Match fastembed's TLS choice: rustls-only so musl/aarch64 cross builds
+# don't need a system OpenSSL toolchain.
+ort = { version = "2.0.0-rc.11", default-features = false, features = ["download-binaries", "tls-rustls"] }
 ndarray = "0.17"
 
 # HNSW approximate nearest neighbors

@@ -10,7 +10,13 @@ repository.workspace = true
 default = ["hash"]
 hash = []
 model2vec = ["dep:safetensors", "dep:tokenizers"]
-fastembed = ["dep:fastembed"]
+# fastembed v5's `hf-hub-rustls-tls` feature does NOT pass
+# default-features = false to hf-hub, so hf-hub still pulls native-tls
+# (openssl-sys) on top of rustls. We pull openssl-sys explicitly with the
+# `vendored` feature so musl/aarch64/intel-darwin cross-compile works
+# without a system OpenSSL toolchain. The dep is gated under the same
+# `fastembed` feature it follows so non-fastembed builds skip it entirely.
+fastembed = ["dep:fastembed", "dep:openssl-sys"]
 download = ["asupersync/tls", "asupersync/tls-webpki-roots"]
 api = ["asupersync/tls", "asupersync/tls-webpki-roots"]
 bundled-default-models = ["model2vec", "fastembed"]
@@ -28,6 +34,9 @@ sha2 = { workspace = true }
 safetensors = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 tokenizers = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 fastembed = { workspace = true, optional = true }
+# Pulled in only when fastembed is enabled — see comment on the
+# `fastembed` feature above for why we vendor openssl.
+openssl-sys = { version = "0.9", features = ["vendored"], optional = true }
 
 [dev-dependencies]
 asupersync = { workspace = true }
